symbols.name: V
symbols.def: Observed-node vertex set of the acyclic directed mixed graph G.

symbols.name: G
symbols.def: Known acyclic directed mixed graph on V, with directed edges E_dir and bidirected edges E_bidir.

symbols.name: u
symbols.def: Parent endpoint of the target directed edge u -> v in G.

symbols.name: v
symbols.def: Child endpoint of the target directed edge u -> v in G.

symbols.name: P_v
symbols.def: Ordered parent tuple P_v = (p_1,...,p_q) = pa(v).

symbols.name: q
symbols.def: Number of parents q = |P_v|.

symbols.name: s_u
symbols.def: Unique index such that p_{s_u} = u in the ordered tuple P_v.

symbols.name: Lambda
symbols.def: Directed-edge coefficient matrix with Lambda_ab = 0 whenever a -> b is absent from G.

symbols.name: B_Lambda
symbols.def: Directed-path transfer matrix B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T).

symbols.name: epsilon
symbols.def: Exogenous error vector in the representation X = B_Lambda epsilon.

symbols.name: X
symbols.def: Observed node vector satisfying X = B_Lambda epsilon.

symbols.name: I_v
symbols.def: Ordered witness tuple I_v = (i_1,...,i_q) used in the TDE non-intersecting-path certificate for P_v.

symbols.name: Pi_v
symbols.def: Non-intersecting directed path system Pi_v = (pi_1,...,pi_q) pairing i_r to p_r.

symbols.name: m
symbols.def: Order of the observable joint cumulants used in the transfer construction.

symbols.name: C
symbols.def: Family C = (C_1,...,C_q) where each C_r is an ordered (m-2)-tuple of observed nodes used to augment row r of the transfer system.

symbols.name: m_prime
symbols.def: Alternative cumulant order used for a second admissible family.

symbols.name: C_prime
symbols.def: Alternative admissible family C_prime = (C_prime_1,...,C_prime_q) used to compare observable transfer designs.

symbols.name: Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)
symbols.def: Set of order-m graph-uniform cumulant-admissible families C that already realize the witness-specific observable row-uniform transfer for the target edge u -> v relative to the witness (I_v,Pi_v).

symbols.name: m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)
symbols.def: Smallest m for which Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty; infinity if no such m exists.

symbols.name: K_X(I_v,C)
symbols.def: Observable transfer matrix with entry [K_X(I_v,C)]_{r,s} = kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r).

symbols.name: k_X(v;I_v,C)
symbols.def: Observable transfer vector with entry [k_X(v;I_v,C)]_r = kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r).

symbols.name: lambda_Pv,v
symbols.def: Parent-effect vector lambda_Pv,v = (Lambda_{p_1 v},...,Lambda_{p_q v})^T.

symbols.name: lambda_uv
symbols.def: Target direct-effect coefficient lambda_uv = Lambda_{uv}.

symbols.name: Khat_n(I_v,C)
symbols.def: Empirical k-statistic analogue of K_X(I_v,C) computed from n i.i.d. observations of X.

symbols.name: khat_n(v;I_v,C)
symbols.def: Empirical k-statistic analogue of k_X(v;I_v,C) computed from n i.i.d. observations of X.

symbols.name: lambdahat_uv,n(C)
symbols.def: Plug-in estimator obtained by replacing the observable cumulants in the determinant ratio for lambda_uv by their empirical k-statistics, using the empirical determinant ratio when det Khat_n(I_v,C) != 0 and the value 0 otherwise.

assumptions.condition: G is an acyclic directed mixed graph on V and u -> v is a directed edge in G.
X = B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T) and Lambda_ab = 0 whenever a -> b is absent from G.
The error vector epsilon satisfies the connected-set Markov property with respect to the bidirected part of G.

standard.name: connected-set Markov property for bidirected errors

assumptions.condition: There exist an ordered witness tuple I_v and a non-intersecting path system Pi_v pairing i_r to p_r for r = 1,...,q, so the TDE v-rank criterion certifies generic identifiability of lambda_uv.
The directed coefficients and the nonzero connected error cumulants lie outside the algebraic exceptional set on which the relevant witness minors vanish.
All joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite.

standard.name: finite high-order moment condition for cumulant CLTs

assumptions.condition: For the chosen admissible pair (m,C), det K_X(I_v,C) is nonzero at the true law; equivalently, |det K_X(I_v,C)| >= c0 for some constant c0 > 0.

definitions.name: Witness-row augmentation search handle
definitions.construction: Starting from the TDE witness (I_v,Pi_v), enumerate m = 2,3,... and families C = (C_1,...,C_q) in (V^{m-2})^q; retain a family when the multilinear cumulant expansion of each row map j -> kappa_X(i_r,j,C_r) under X = B_Lambda epsilon leaves exactly one nonvanishing connected-error contribution across j in P_v union {v}, so the same row scalar multiplies every column.

definitions.name: Order-m graph-uniform cumulant-admissible family (conditional transfer design)
definitions.construction: { C = (C_1,...,C_q) in (V^{m-2})^q : outside a proper algebraic subset of parameter pairs (Lambda,epsilon) satisfying ass:linear-mixed-representation and ass:connected-set-markov on G, the witness-specific observable row-uniform transfer already holds, meaning that for each r in {1,...,q} there exists a nonzero scalar A_r(Lambda,epsilon) independent of s such that kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} for every s in {1,...,q} and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} }

definitions.name: Minimal graph-uniform admissible cumulant order
definitions.construction: m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) = min{ m >= 2 : Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) != emptyset }, with value infinity if the set is empty.

definitions.name: Sample cumulant plug-in estimator
definitions.construction: lambdahat_uv,n(C) = det(Khat_n(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by khat_n(v;I_v,C)) / det Khat_n(I_v,C) when det Khat_n(I_v,C) != 0, and lambdahat_uv,n(C) = 0 otherwise.

statements.statement: Under ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity, does the search handle in def:transfer-search-handle always terminate with some finite order m and some family C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)?
statements.justification: This node calibrates the note's own witness-specific search handle rather than carrying the note's field claim by itself: without it, the conditional transfer kernel would be easy to overread as a blanket existence theorem on the whole TDE frontier.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 supplies the generic identifiability certificate and the hidden row system in Eq. (3.9), but it does not promise that an observable row-uniform lift exists automatically for every witness; KivvaSalehkaleybarKiyavash2023 and TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 instead build hand-tailored moment equations on narrower proxy or underspecified-IV subclasses.
statements.consumer: The counterexample tells the orchestrator and any search procedure that admissible-family existence is separate work: a TDE witness alone does not justify launching a finite observable transfer search as though success were automatic.
statements.proof_tex:
Negative resolution. The search handle does not always terminate from the frozen assumptions. It suffices to give one acyclic ADMG satisfying the TDE witness condition for which no finite admissible family exists. Let V={1,2,3,4}. Let the directed edges be 1->2, 1->3, 2->4, and 3->4, and let the bidirected edges be 1<->2, 2<->4, and 3<->4. Take the target edge u->v to be 2->4. Order the parent tuple as P_4=(3,2), so q=2 and s_u=2. Let I_4=(1,2), with path system Pi_4 consisting of the directed path 1->3 and the trivial path 2->2. The graph is acyclic. Moreover Sib(4)={2,3,4}, Sib(1)={1,2}, Sib(2)={1,2,4}, and Sib(3)={3,4}, so the removable-ancestor set for node 4 in the TDE criterion is R_4={1,2}. The displayed paths are vertex-disjoint and pair I_4 to P_4. Equivalently, after writing a=lambda_12, b=lambda_13, c=lambda_24, and d=lambda_34, the hidden witness submatrix B_{P_4,I_4} is [[b,0],[a,1]], whose determinant is b and is generically nonzero. Thus this instance satisfies the body of ass:tde-witness, and the TDE v-rank criterion certifies generic identifiability of the two parent coefficients of node 4, in particular lambda_24.

Keep the notation a=lambda_12, b=lambda_13, c=lambda_24, and d=lambda_34. From X=B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda=(I-Lambda)^(-T), the directed-path transfer matrix is

B_Lambda = [[1,0,0,0],[a,1,0,0],[b,0,1,0],[ac+bd,c,d,1]].

In particular B_{3,2}=0, while B_{w,1} is the polynomial 1, a, b, or ac+bd according as w=1,2,3,4. Each of these four polynomials is generically nonzero after excluding a proper algebraic set.

Now fix an arbitrary cumulant order m>=2 and an arbitrary candidate row augmentation C_2=(c_1,...,c_{m-2}) in V^{m-2} for the second witness row i_2=2. Consider the column j=3. By multilinearity of joint cumulants and X_w=sum_t B_{w,t} epsilon_t,

kappa_X(2,3,C_2) = sum_{t_0,...,t_{m-1} in V} B_{2,t_0} B_{3,t_1} prod_{ell=1}^{m-2} B_{c_ell,t_{ell+1}} kappa_epsilon(t_0,t_1,t_2,...,t_{m-1}).

The summand with t_0=t_1=...=t_{m-1}=1 is

a b prod_{ell=1}^{m-2} B_{c_ell,1} kappa_epsilon(1,1,...,1).

The singleton set {1} is connected in the bidirected part, so ass:connected-set-markov imposes no zero restriction on the cumulant coordinate kappa_epsilon(1,1,...,1). The coefficient a b prod_ell B_{c_ell,1} is a nonzero polynomial in a,b,c,d. Hence kappa_X(2,3,C_2) is not the zero polynomial in the directed coefficients and connected error cumulants; it is nonzero on a Zariski-open generic subset of the model class.

But if C belonged to Mcal_m(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4), then the second row of def:cumulant-admissible-family would require, for the parent p_1=3,

kappa_X(i_2,p_1,C_2) = A_2(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{p_1,i_2} = A_2(Lambda,epsilon) B_{3,2} = 0.

This contradicts the generic nonvanishing just proved. Since the argument used an arbitrary order m and an arbitrary C_2 in V^{m-2}, no family C can be admissible at any finite order. Therefore Mcal_m(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4) is empty for every m>=2, so m_star(G,2,4,I_4,Pi_4)=infinity for this TDE-certified instance. Thus the answer to the open-ended question is no: ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity do not force the search handle in def:transfer-search-handle to terminate.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and let C belong to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Then, generically, K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C) and det K_X(I_v,C) != 0.
statements.justification: This is the core conditional transfer template: it says what any successful admissible family buys once supplied, not that every TDE-certified edge automatically has such a family.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 identifies the hidden B_Lambda witness system around Eq. (3.9), while KivvaSalehkaleybarKiyavash2023 and TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 hand-build observable moment equations only on narrower subclasses; the covariance-layer determinant systems in FoygelDraismaDrton2012 and BarberDrtonSturmaWeihs2022 do not address higher-order row-uniform cumulant lifts under general non-factor confounding.
statements.consumer: The row system is the reusable kernel inside any subclass theorem or future existence result that actually supplies an admissible family, because it turns that supplied design into simultaneous recovery of the whole parent-effect vector.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Work on the generic subset on which the admissibility factorization for C holds and the TDE witness minor is nonzero. Let H be the q by q hidden witness-transfer matrix H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}, and let b be the q-vector b_r = (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. By lem:admissible-row-factorization, there are nonzero row scalars A_1,...,A_q such that K_X(I_v,C) = D_A H and k_X(v;I_v,C) = D_A b, where D_A = diag(A_1,...,A_q). By lem:witness-transfer-identity, H lambda_Pv,v = b. Therefore K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = D_A H lambda_Pv,v = D_A b = k_X(v;I_v,C). It remains to check nonsingularity. By lem:witness-minor-invertible, det H != 0 generically. Since every A_r is nonzero on the same generic set, det K_X(I_v,C) = det(D_A H) = (prod_{r=1}^q A_r) det H != 0. This proves both the observable row system and the generic denominator condition.

statements.statement: Under the conditions of thm:cumulant-row-system, lambda_uv equals det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) divided by det K_X(I_v,C).
statements.justification: This is the conditional kernel: once an admissible observable transfer family has already been justified, it turns the hidden TDE row identity into an explicit determinant ratio for the target direct effect.
statements.gap: KivvaSalehkaleybarKiyavash2023 gives a cross-moment ratio for a single-proxy graph, and TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 gives higher-order cumulant formulas for the proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM setups in Sections 3.1 and 3.2; neither paper supplies a general observable determinant ratio for an arbitrary TDE witness conditional on a row-uniform admissible transfer design.
statements.consumer: The ratio is the algebraic engine used whenever an admissible family is available, and it is the formula instantiated unconditionally on narrower subclasses such as thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Under thm:cumulant-row-system, K_X(I_v,C) is nonsingular and K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C). Hence lambda_Pv,v is the unique solution x of K_X(I_v,C)x = k_X(v;I_v,C). Expanding det(K_X with column s replaced by k_X) along the standard alternating multilinear determinant in the s-th column, or equivalently applying Cramer's rule, gives x_s = det(K_X with column s replaced by k_X)/det K_X for every s = 1,...,q. Taking s = s_u and using p_{s_u}=u, we obtain x_{s_u} = Lambda_{u v} = lambda_uv. Thus lambda_uv equals det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) divided by det K_X(I_v,C).

statements.statement: If C belongs to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) and C_prime belongs to Mcal_{m_prime}(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), then the determinant ratios in thm:closed-form-coefficient coincide as rational functions on the generic model class.
statements.justification: The identifying functional should depend on the edge and witness, not on a numerically convenient augmentation choice.
statements.gap: Neither the hidden witness formalism in TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 nor the special-case cumulant identities in TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 establish agreement across distinct observable admissible families.
statements.consumer: Witness invariance permits software to select a stable admissible family without changing the estimand that is reported or the target of inference.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let R_C=N_C/D_C be the determinant ratio built from the admissible family C, and let R_{C'}=N_{C'}/D_{C'} be the analogous ratio built from C'. On the generic domain where D_C != 0, thm:closed-form-coefficient gives R_C = lambda_uv. On the generic domain where D_{C'} != 0, the same theorem gives R_{C'} = lambda_uv. The intersection of these two generic domains is again nonempty and Zariski-open, since it is obtained by deleting the union of two proper algebraic exceptional sets. Hence R_C = R_{C'} on that intersection.

Now pull everything back to the model parameter space with coordinates given by the free directed coefficients and the free connected error cumulants of the bounded orders appearing in C and C'. Each observable cumulant entering N_C, D_C, N_{C'}, and D_{C'} is a polynomial in those coordinates, so N_C, D_C, N_{C'}, and D_{C'} are themselves polynomials. On the common domain D_C D_{C'} != 0, the equality R_C = R_{C'} is equivalent to the polynomial identity N_C D_{C'} - N_{C'} D_C = 0. This polynomial vanishes on the nonempty Zariski-open intersection above, hence it is the zero polynomial. Therefore N_C D_{C'} = N_{C'} D_C identically, which is exactly the statement that R_C and R_{C'} are the same rational function on the generic model class. Thus the determinant ratios coincide as rational functions on the generic model class.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Under ass:finite-moments and ass:denominator-margin, lambdahat_uv,n(C) converges in probability to lambda_uv and sqrt(n) (lambdahat_uv,n(C) - lambda_uv) converges in distribution to a mean-zero normal law whose variance is obtained by the multivariate delta method applied to the vector of sample cumulants entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C).
statements.justification: A constructive formula is materially stronger once it supports standard pointwise plug-in inference at a denominator-nonzero true law for a fixed admissible design.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 explicitly leaves confidence intervals open, and MareisSturmaDrton2026 gives the analogous asymptotic normality result only for covariance half-trek estimators rather than higher-order cumulant ratios under general confounding.
statements.consumer: This proposition yields plug-in standard errors and Wald intervals after a concrete admissible cumulant design has been fixed and the chosen true law lies away from the denominator-zero locus.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix the admissible pair (m,C). Let T be the finite vector of all distinct population cumulants kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) entering K_X(I_v,C) and k_X(v;I_v,C), and let T_hat_n be the corresponding vector of empirical k-statistics entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C). Let g be the rational map that sends a cumulant vector t to det(K(t) with column s_u replaced by k(t))/det K(t) whenever det K(t) != 0, and define the total map g_0 by g_0(t)=g(t) on that domain and g_0(t)=0 when det K(t)=0. By def:plugin-estimator, lambdahat_uv,n(C)=g_0(T_hat_n).

By ass:denominator-margin, det K(T) is nonzero at the true law. This proposition uses only that pointwise nonvanishing, not any stronger quantitative margin. Since t -> det K(t) is continuous, there is an open neighborhood U of T on which det K(t) stays nonzero, so g_0 agrees with g on U and g is continuously differentiable there. By lem:sample-cumulant-clt-delta and ass:finite-moments, T_hat_n ->p T and sqrt(n)(T_hat_n-T) ->d N(0,Sigma_T), where Sigma_T is the limiting covariance matrix of the empirical cumulant vector. Therefore P(T_hat_n in U) -> 1, and on that event g_0(T_hat_n)=g(T_hat_n).

By continuity of g on U, g_0(T_hat_n)=g(T_hat_n) ->p g(T). It remains to identify g(T) at the true law. Let N_C and D_C denote the determinant numerator and denominator from thm:closed-form-coefficient. By lem:closed-form-ratio-specializes-pointwise, the polynomial identity N_C = lambda_uv D_C holds on the full model parameter space. Evaluating at the true law and using D_C(T)=det K(T) != 0 gives g(T)=N_C(T)/D_C(T)=lambda_uv. Hence lambdahat_uv,n(C) ->p lambda_uv.

The same finite-dimensional delta-method lemma applied to g on U yields
sqrt(n)(g(T_hat_n)-g(T)) ->d N(0, grad g(T)^T Sigma_T grad g(T)).
Since g_0(T_hat_n)=g(T_hat_n) with probability tending to one, the same limit holds with g_0(T_hat_n) in place of g(T_hat_n). Substituting g(T)=lambda_uv from the preceding paragraph gives
sqrt(n)(lambdahat_uv,n(C)-lambda_uv) ->d N(0, grad g(T)^T Sigma_T grad g(T)).
Therefore sqrt(n)(lambdahat_uv,n(C)-lambda_uv) converges in distribution to a mean-zero normal law with variance grad g(T)^T Sigma_T grad g(T), precisely the multivariate delta-method variance for the sample cumulants entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C).

statements.statement: If q = 1, P_v = (u), and the empty family C = (empty tuple) belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), then thm:closed-form-coefficient reduces to lambda_uv = Cov(X_{i_1}, X_v) / Cov(X_{i_1}, X_u).
statements.justification: The general cumulant formula should collapse to the classical covariance IV ratio when a second-moment witness exists.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 uses the covariance IV ratio as a motivating example, but does not embed it as the order-2 special case of a graph-uniform cumulant transfer theorem.
statements.consumer: This reduction is the referee-checkable sanity benchmark separating genuinely higher-order identification from the familiar IV case.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. If q = 1 and P_v = (u), then s_u = 1, K_X(I_v,C) is the 1 by 1 matrix with entry kappa_X(i_1,u,C_1), and k_X(v;I_v,C) is the one-vector with entry kappa_X(i_1,v,C_1). For m = 2 and the empty tuple C_1, these entries are kappa_X(i_1,u) and kappa_X(i_1,v). The determinant ratio in thm:closed-form-coefficient is therefore kappa_X(i_1,v)/kappa_X(i_1,u). By lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance, kappa_X(i_1,a) = Cov(X_{i_1},X_a) for a in {u,v}. Hence the order-2 formula is lambda_uv = Cov(X_{i_1},X_v)/Cov(X_{i_1},X_u), with the denominator nonzero on the generic domain supplied by thm:closed-form-coefficient.

statements.statement: Suppose ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity hold, and in addition each witness row i_r (r=1,...,q) is a directed SOURCE in G with An(i_r)={i_r} and is bidirected-disconnected from every error node that is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}. Then the empty order-2 family C with C_r = empty tuple for all r belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v); concretely K_X(I_v,C) = diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q)) H with H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and k_X(v;I_v,C)=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r)) b with b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. Consequently, on the generic model class, det K_X(I_v,C) != 0 and lambda_Pv,v = K_X(I_v,C)^{-1} k_X(v;I_v,C), so by thm:closed-form-coefficient the entire parent-effect vector of v, in particular lambda_uv, is identified by the observable ORDER-2 (covariance) determinant ratio. Hence on the source-isolated witness subclass the observable identifying functional exists UNCONDITIONALLY at covariance order (m_star=2), and prop:iv-reduction is the q=1 special case.
statements.justification: This is the note's strongest unconditional positive theorem and its main field-facing claim: it isolates a TDE witness subclass where the observable lift already exists at covariance order, rather than merely reusing determinant algebra after admissibility has been assumed.
statements.gap: The closest comparators are classical one-row IV ratios and covariance half-trek determinant formulas. Those works provide the determinant-ratio template, but they do not isolate this source-disconnected multi-parent TDE subclass together with an admissibility proof for the empty order-2 family showing that the TDE observable lift lands exactly in that covariance comparator class.
statements.consumer: The theorem tells the reader when existing covariance-layer determinant machinery is already enough to recover the whole parent-effect vector and when a genuinely higher-order cumulant search is still needed.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Work on the source-isolated subclass described in the statement, and let C be the order-2 empty family, so C_r is the empty tuple for every r. By lem:source-isolated-empty-family-admissible, this C belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v); in particular the row scalars kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r) are nonzero on a generic subset and the empty covariance family is genuinely admissible. The same lemma gives the advertised observable row factorization: if H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}, b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}, and D=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q)), then K_X(I_v,C)=D H and k_X(v;I_v,C)=D b. This proves the concrete covariance-transfer display in the theorem.

Since m=2 and C is admissible, thm:cumulant-row-system applies on the generic model class. It yields K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C) and det K_X(I_v,C) != 0. Therefore K_X(I_v,C) is invertible and lambda_Pv,v = K_X(I_v,C)^{-1} k_X(v;I_v,C). This identifies the whole parent-effect vector from observable second cumulants.

For the target coefficient, apply thm:closed-form-coefficient to the same admissible pair (2,C). The s_u coordinate of lambda_Pv,v is lambda_uv because p_{s_u}=u, hence
lambda_uv = det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) / det K_X(I_v,C).
All entries in this determinant ratio are observable order-2 cumulants, equivalently covariances by lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance whenever the second moments are finite.

By lem:order-two-admissible-implies-minimal-order-two, the nonemptiness of Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) implies m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)=2, because the search domain begins at order 2. Finally suppose q=1 and P_v=(u). Then K_X(I_v,C) is the 1 by 1 matrix [kappa_X(i_1,u)] and k_X(v;I_v,C) is [kappa_X(i_1,v)]. The determinant ratio becomes kappa_X(i_1,v)/kappa_X(i_1,u), which equals Cov(X_{i_1},X_v)/Cov(X_{i_1},X_u) by lem:second-cumulant-is-covariance. Thus prop:iv-reduction is exactly the one-parent special case of this source-isolated covariance theorem.

statements.statement: Characterize m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) from the bidirected connected-set pattern and the witness Pi_v, and determine whether for every m < m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) there exist two generic laws in the model class that agree on all observable cumulants up to order m yet differ in lambda_uv.
statements.justification: Higher-order cumulants are statistically expensive, so the note has to clarify what the witness-specific quantity m_star does and does not measure once the hoped-for sharp-frontier interpretation fails.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 justifies genericity through cumulants of arbitrary order, and SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 builds graph-dependent cumulant rank matrices, but neither paper separates witness-specific transfer order from a genuinely information-theoretic lower identification order.
statements.consumer: This node now serves as a calibration result: it explains why lower-order failure of a chosen witness transfer design does not by itself imply lower-order impossibility for identifying lambda_uv.
statements.proof_tex:
The requested sharpness statement has a negative answer under the frozen core. The obstruction is that \(m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\) is a witness-specific transfer order, while lower-order observable cumulants may identify \(\lambda_{uv}\) by a different witness or a different formula. Thus \(m_\star\) is not, in general, an information-theoretic lower frontier for all cumulant functionals.

The concrete counterexample is `lem:chosen-witness-order-not-information-frontier`. In that graph the chosen TDE witness is \(I_v=(w)\) for the target edge \(u	o v\). The order-two empty augmentation is not admissible for this chosen witness, because the bidirected edge \(w\leftrightarrow v\) contributes the nonzero covariance term \(\kappa_\epsilon(w,v)\) to \(\kappa_X(w,v)\) without contributing to \(B_{v,w}\). Hence \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)=\emptyset\), so \(2<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\) with the convention that every finite integer is smaller than \(\infty\).

At the same time the same graph contains a second observed node \(z\) with directed path \(z	o u	o v\) and no bidirected connection to the remaining nodes. For every generic law in this model,
\[
\kappa_X(z,u)=\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,\qquad
\kappa_X(z,v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,
\]
with \(\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2
e 0\). Therefore
\[
\lambda_{uv}= rac{\kappa_X(z,v)}{\kappa_X(z,u)}.
\]
This ratio uses only order-two observable cumulants. Consequently, two generic laws on this graph that agree on all observable cumulants up to order two must have the same value of \(\lambda_{uv}\). The proposed lower-order indistinguishability clause, applied at \(m=2<m_\star\), is false.

What remains universally true is only the construction-level statement built into `def:minimal-order`: if \(h<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), then no order-\(h\) family belongs to \(\mathcal M_h(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\). This follows directly from the definition of the minimum over the nonempty admissible-order set, with the same convention when the set is empty and \(m_\star=\infty\). But this conclusion rules out only the row-uniform cumulant-transfer design associated with the chosen witness. It does not rule out other order-\(h\) observable rational functionals, and therefore it cannot yield the requested two-law indistinguishability result.

Thus the answer to the OEQ is: no, the frozen \(m_\star\) cannot be characterized as a sharp lower cumulant-identification order for \(\lambda_{uv}\) from the stated primitives. A true sharp-order theorem would need a different object, namely an information order minimizing over all observable cumulant functionals or an additional global lower-bound theorem; neither is contained in `def:minimal-order` or the stated assumptions.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Generically there exist nonzero scalars A_1,...,A_q such that, with H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and b_r = (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}, K_X(I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q) H and k_X(v;I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q) b.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Since C belongs to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), def:cumulant-admissible-family applies. Outside the proper algebraic exceptional set in that definition, for each r there is a nonzero scalar A_r(Lambda,epsilon), independent of s, such that kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) = A_r(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} for every s and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) = A_r(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. By def:observable-transfer-matrix, the left side of the first equality is [K_X(I_v,C)]_{r,s}; by def:observable-transfer-vector, the left side of the second equality is [k_X(v;I_v,C)]_r. Thus the r-th row of K_X(I_v,C) is A_r times the r-th row of H, and the r-th entry of k_X(v;I_v,C) is A_r b_r. This is exactly K_X(I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q)H and k_X(v;I_v,C) = diag(A_1,...,A_q)b.

statements.statement: Under ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, and ass:tde-witness, if H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and b_r = (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}, then H lambda_Pv,v = b.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. From ass:linear-mixed-representation, B_Lambda = (I-Lambda)^(-T), so (I-Lambda)^T B_Lambda = I. Taking row v and column i_r gives (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} - sum_{a in V} Lambda_{a v}(B_Lambda)_{a,i_r} = 1{v=i_r}. By the graph sparsity in ass:linear-mixed-representation, Lambda_{a v}=0 unless a is a parent of v, so the sum reduces to a in pa(v) = {p_1,...,p_q}. We now show that 1{v=i_r}=0. The TDE witness gives a directed path pi_r from i_r to p_r, and p_r is a parent of v. If i_r=v, then pi_r followed by the directed edge p_r -> v would form a directed cycle; if pi_r is the length-zero path, then p_r=i_r=v would make v a parent of itself, also impossible in an acyclic directed mixed graph. Thus i_r != v. Therefore (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} = sum_{s=1}^q Lambda_{p_s v}(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}. This is precisely b_r = (H lambda_Pv,v)_r for every r, and hence H lambda_Pv,v = b.

statements.statement: Under ass:tde-witness and ass:genericity, the hidden witness-transfer matrix H with entries H_{r,s} = (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} satisfies det H != 0 generically.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. The TDE witness consists of a non-intersecting directed path system Pi_v pairing i_r to p_r. The associated v-rank witness minor is the determinant of the submatrix of B_Lambda with columns indexed by the witness nodes I_v and rows indexed by the parents P_v, equivalently det H up to transpose. Ass:genericity states that the directed coefficients and nonzero connected error cumulants lie outside the algebraic exceptional set on which the relevant witness minors vanish. Applying this to the witness minor supplied by Pi_v yields det H != 0 on the generic model class.

statements.statement: For any two coordinates a and b for which the second cumulant is finite, kappa_X(a,b) = Cov(X_a,X_b).
statements.proof_tex: Proof. The second joint cumulant of two scalar random variables is cum(Y,Z) = E[YZ] - E[Y]E[Z]. Taking Y = X_a and Z = X_b gives kappa_X(a,b) = E[X_a X_b] - E[X_a]E[X_b] = Cov(X_a,X_b).

statements.statement: There is an acyclic directed mixed graph satisfying the frozen structural assumptions and a chosen TDE witness (I_v,Pi_v) for which Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is empty, hence 2 < m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), while lambda_uv is identified from order-two observable cumulants. Therefore the witness-specific minimal transfer order m_star is not a lower-order indistinguishability frontier.
statements.proof_tex:
Let \(V_H=\{z,w,u,v\}\). Put directed edges \(z	o u\), \(w	o u\), and \(u	o v\), and put the single bidirected edge \(w\leftrightarrow v\). There are no other directed or bidirected edges. The directed part is acyclic. The target edge is \(u	o v\), so \(P_v=(u)\), \(q=1\), and we choose the witness tuple \(I_v=(w)\) with path system \(\Pi_v=(w	o u)\). For \(q=1\), the nonintersecting-path condition is just the existence of a directed path from the witness to the parent, and generically \((B_\Lambda)_{u,w}=\lambda_{wu}
e 0\), so this is a valid TDE rank witness.

Take the linear SEM
\[
X_z=\epsilon_z,\qquad X_w=\epsilon_w,\qquad X_u=\lambda_{zu}X_z+\lambda_{wu}X_w+\epsilon_u,\qquad X_v=\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v.
\]
Choose finite-moment error laws satisfying the connected-set Markov property for the bidirected graph: the only off-diagonal second cumulant allowed to be generically nonzero is \(\omega_{wv}:=\kappa_\epsilon(w,v)\), while \(z\) is bidirected-disconnected from every other node and \(u\) is bidirected-isolated. Work on the generic open set where
\[
\lambda_{zu}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_z^2\sigma_w^2\omega_{wv}
e 0,\qquad\sigma_z^2=\kappa_\epsilon(z,z),\quad \sigma_w^2=\kappa_\epsilon(w,w).
\]
The path-transfer entries needed below are
\[
(B_\Lambda)_{u,w}=\lambda_{wu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{v,w}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{u,z}=\lambda_{zu},\qquad (B_\Lambda)_{v,z}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}.
\]

First show that the order-two empty augmentation is not admissible for the chosen witness \(w\). Since \(m=2\) and \(q=1\), the only candidate family is \(C=(\emptyset)\). By multilinearity of cumulants and the connected-set Markov zeros,
\[
\kappa_X(w,u)=\kappa(\epsilon_w,\lambda_{zu}\epsilon_z+\lambda_{wu}\epsilon_w+\epsilon_u)=\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2,
\]
and
\[
\kappa_X(w,v)=\kappa(\epsilon_w,\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2+\omega_{wv}.
\]
If \(C=(\emptyset)\) belonged to \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), `def:cumulant-admissible-family` would give a scalar \(A
e0\) such that
\[
\kappa_X(w,u)=A(B_\Lambda)_{u,w},\qquad \kappa_X(w,v)=A(B_\Lambda)_{v,w}.
\]
The first equality and \(\lambda_{wu}
e0\) force \(A=\sigma_w^2\). The second equality would then force
\[
\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2+\omega_{wv}=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{wu}\sigma_w^2,
\]
so \(\omega_{wv}=0\), contradicting the generic choice. Hence \(C=(\emptyset)
otin\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\). Since it is the only order-two family, \(\mathcal M_2(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)=\emptyset\), and by `def:minimal-order` we have \(2<m_\star(G,u,v,I_v,\Pi_v)\), allowing \(m_\star=\infty\).

Now show that order-two observable cumulants nevertheless identify the target coefficient on the same graph. Again by multilinearity and the connected-set Markov zeros involving \(z\),
\[
\kappa_X(z,u)=\kappa(\epsilon_z,\lambda_{zu}\epsilon_z+\lambda_{wu}\epsilon_w+\epsilon_u)=\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2,
\]
and
\[
\kappa_X(z,v)=\kappa(\epsilon_z,\lambda_{uv}X_u+\epsilon_v)=\lambda_{uv}\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2.
\]
On the generic set \(\lambda_{zu}\sigma_z^2
e0\),
\[
\lambda_{uv}= rac{\kappa_X(z,v)}{\kappa_X(z,u)}.
\]
Therefore any two generic laws in this model that agree on all observable cumulants up to order two agree in particular on \(\kappa_X(z,u)\) and \(\kappa_X(z,v)\), and the displayed ratio forces them to have the same \(\lambda_{uv}\). Thus no pair of generic laws can agree on all order-two observable cumulants and differ in \(\lambda_{uv}\). This proves that the chosen-witness minimal transfer order is not an information-theoretic lower frontier.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Let N_C and D_C be the determinant numerator and denominator from thm:closed-form-coefficient. Then N_C = lambda_uv D_C as a polynomial identity on the model parameter space. Consequently, at every law with D_C != 0, the determinant ratio equals lambda_uv.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Let N_C and D_C denote the determinant numerator and denominator from thm:closed-form-coefficient. On the generic domain where D_C != 0, that theorem gives N_C / D_C = lambda_uv. Pull this identity back to the model parameter space with coordinates given by the free directed coefficients together with the free connected error cumulants of the bounded orders appearing in C. Each observable cumulant entering N_C and D_C is a polynomial in those coordinates, so N_C and D_C are polynomials, while lambda_uv is one of the coordinate functions. On the generic domain D_C != 0, the displayed equality is equivalent to the polynomial identity N_C - lambda_uv D_C = 0. This polynomial vanishes on a nonempty Zariski-open subset of the parameter space, hence it is the zero polynomial. Therefore N_C = lambda_uv D_C identically. Evaluating at any law with D_C != 0 gives N_C / D_C = lambda_uv there as well.

statements.statement: In an acyclic linear mixed representation X = B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T), the entry (B_Lambda)_{j,t} is zero unless t is a directed ancestor of j, and (B_Lambda)_{j,j}=1. Consequently, if An(i)={i}, then X_i=epsilon_i.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Because the directed part of G is acyclic, choose a topological order. In that order the directed-edge matrix is nilpotent, so
B_Lambda=(I-Lambda)^(-T)=(I-Lambda^T)^(-1)=sum_{ell=0}^{|V|-1} (Lambda^T)^ell.
For ell >= 1, the (j,t) entry of (Lambda^T)^ell is a sum over directed paths of length ell from t to j, with each path weighted by the product of its directed coefficients. Thus no directed path from t to j implies (B_Lambda)_{j,t}=0. The ell=0 term is the identity matrix, so (B_Lambda)_{j,j}=1. If An(i)={i}, then no t != i has a directed path to i, hence X_i=sum_t (B_Lambda)_{i,t} epsilon_t=epsilon_i.

statements.statement: Assume the source-isolation condition in thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible: each i_r is a directed source with An(i_r)={i_r} and, for every distinct t that is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}, i_r is bidirected-disconnected from t. Then for every r and every j in P_v union {v}, kappa_X(i_r,j)=kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r)(B_Lambda)_{j,i_r} on the model class where the displayed second cumulants are defined.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix r and j in P_v union {v}. By lem:acyclic-transfer-support, X_{i_r}=epsilon_{i_r}. Also X_j=sum_{t in V} (B_Lambda)_{j,t} epsilon_t. Bilinearity of the second cumulant gives
kappa_X(i_r,j)=kappa(epsilon_{i_r}, sum_t (B_Lambda)_{j,t} epsilon_t)=sum_t (B_Lambda)_{j,t} kappa_epsilon(i_r,t).
Again by lem:acyclic-transfer-support, (B_Lambda)_{j,t}=0 unless t is a directed ancestor of j. Since j belongs to P_v union {v}, every t with a possibly nonzero coefficient is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}. For t != i_r, the source-isolation condition says that i_r and t are bidirected-disconnected. The connected-set Markov property for bidirected errors says that a mixed error cumulant indexed by a bidirected-disconnected set is zero; at order two this gives kappa_epsilon(i_r,t)=0 for every such t != i_r. Therefore the sum has only the t=i_r contribution, namely kappa_X(i_r,j)=(B_Lambda)_{j,i_r} kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r).

statements.statement: Under ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, ass:genericity, and the source-isolation condition in thm:source-isolated-covariance-admissible, the empty order-2 family C with C_r empty for all r belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Moreover K_X(I_v,C)=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q))H with H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r}, and k_X(v;I_v,C)=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r))b with b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let C_r be the empty tuple for every r, so C is an order-2 family, and define A_r=kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r). Because the singleton vertex {i_r} is connected in the bidirected graph, ass:connected-set-markov imposes no vanishing equation on the second cumulant coordinate A_r. In the model parameterization by free directed coefficients together with free connected error cumulants, A_r is therefore a coordinate function, not the zero polynomial. Hence the locus {A_r=0} is a proper algebraic hypersurface. Intersect the generic witness-minor set from ass:genericity with the complements of these finitely many hypersurfaces for r=1,...,q; the result is still a nonempty Zariski-open subset of the model class on which every A_r is nonzero.

Apply lem:source-isolated-second-cumulant-factorization first with j=p_s for s=1,...,q and then with j=v. We obtain
kappa_X(i_r,p_s)=A_r (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r},
kappa_X(i_r,v)=A_r (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}.
The scalar A_r depends on r and on the model parameters, but it is independent of the parent column s. On the generic subset just described, these equalities are exactly the row-uniform factorization requirements in def:cumulant-admissible-family for the empty order-2 family, including the required nonvanishing of every A_r. Hence C belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v).

The displayed matrix identities now follow from def:observable-transfer-matrix and def:observable-transfer-vector: the (r,s) entry of K_X(I_v,C) is kappa_X(i_r,p_s)=A_r H_{r,s}, and the r-th entry of k_X(v;I_v,C) is kappa_X(i_r,v)=A_r b_r.

statements.statement: Fix a finite list of joint cumulants of coordinates of X, each of order at most m, and let T be the population cumulant vector and T_hat_n the corresponding vector of empirical k-statistics computed from n i.i.d. observations of X. Under ass:finite-moments, T_hat_n converges in probability to T and sqrt(n)(T_hat_n-T) converges in distribution to a mean-zero multivariate normal vector. Moreover, if g is continuously differentiable in a neighborhood of T, then sqrt(n)(g(T_hat_n)-g(T)) converges in distribution to the mean-zero normal law with variance Dg(T) Sigma_T Dg(T)^T, where Sigma_T is the covariance matrix of the limiting sample-cumulant vector.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let the finitely many cumulant arguments be J_1,...,J_d, with |J_l| <= m. For every block B appearing in a partition of any J_l, introduce the raw moment mu_B=E[prod_{a in B} X_a]. Only finitely many such raw moments are needed, and each has total degree at most m. Let mu be the resulting finite raw-moment vector and let mu_hat_n be its empirical analogue formed from the n i.i.d. observations of X.

The joint cumulant of an ordered tuple J is the finite partition polynomial
\[
\kappa_X(J)=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi(J)} (|\pi|-1)!(-1)^{|\pi|-1}\prod_{B\in\pi} E\left[\prod_{a\in B}X_a\right].
\]
Thus the population cumulant vector is F(mu) for a polynomial map F. Ass:finite-moments states that all joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite. Hence every monomial entering mu has a finite second moment, because its square has total degree at most 2m. The ordinary finite-dimensional law of large numbers and central limit theorem for the empirical average vector mu_hat_n therefore give
\[
mu_hat_n ->p mu, \qquad \sqrt n(mu_hat_n-mu) ->d N(0,Omega)
\]
for the finite covariance matrix Omega of the raw-moment monomial vector.

The plug-in cumulant vector F(mu_hat_n) is consequently consistent, and the first-order Taylor expansion of the polynomial F gives
\[
\sqrt n\{F(mu_hat_n)-F(mu)\} ->d N(0, DF(mu)Omega DF(mu)^T).
\]
It remains only to pass from plug-in cumulants to the empirical k-statistics named in the frozen core. For fixed cumulant order, each k-statistic is a finite-sample unbiased polynomial in the empirical power sums whose coefficients are rational functions of n and whose coefficientwise limit is the corresponding plug-in cumulant polynomial. Therefore, for the fixed finite list J_1,...,J_d, the difference between the k-statistic vector T_hat_n and F(mu_hat_n) is O_p(n^{-1}); the empirical raw moments appearing in those polynomials are O_p(1) by the preceding law of large numbers. Hence sqrt(n){T_hat_n-F(mu_hat_n)} ->p 0. Slutsky's theorem gives T_hat_n ->p T and
\[
\sqrt n(T_hat_n-T) ->d N(0,Sigma_T),\qquad Sigma_T=DF(mu)Omega DF(mu)^T.
\]
Finally, if g is continuously differentiable in a neighborhood of T, Taylor's formula gives g(T_hat_n)-g(T)=Dg(T)(T_hat_n-T)+o_p(||T_hat_n-T||). Since T_hat_n-T=O_p(n^{-1/2}), multiplying by sqrt(n) and applying Slutsky's theorem yields the stated delta-method normal limit with variance Dg(T) Sigma_T Dg(T)^T.

statements.statement: If Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, then m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)=2.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. By def:minimal-order, m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is the minimum over the set of integers m >= 2 for which Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, with value infinity only if this set is empty. If Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty, then 2 belongs to this set. No integer smaller than 2 belongs to the search domain. Hence the minimum is exactly 2.

sampling_model.design: i.i.d. observational sampling from the law of X in the linear non-Gaussian ADMG model
sampling_model.units: n independent draws X^(1),...,X^(n) of the full observed node vector X
sampling_model.treatment: not design-based; the target is the structural edge coefficient on u -> v
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: No nonparametric nuisance regression is estimated; the observable ingredients are the joint cumulants entering a chosen admissible pair (m,C), estimated by unbiased k-statistics.

estimand_functional: lambda_uv = det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) / det K_X(I_v,C)

bibliography.citation: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, and Jalal Etesami (2024). Parameter identification in linear non-Gaussian causal models under general confounding. arXiv:2405.20856.
Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, and Negar Kiyavash (2023). A Cross-Moment Approach for Causal Effect Estimation. arXiv:2306.06263.
Daniele Tramontano, Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Mathias Drton, and Negar Kiyavash (2025). Causal Effect Identification in lvLiNGAM from Higher-Order Cumulants. ICML 2025; arXiv:2506.05202.
Daniela Schkoda, Elina Robeva, and Mathias Drton (2024). Causal Discovery of Linear Non-Gaussian Causal Models with Unobserved Confounding. arXiv:2408.04907.
Rina Foygel, Jan Draisma, and Mathias Drton (2012). Half-trek criterion for generic identifiability of linear structural equation models. Annals of Statistics 40(3):1682-1713.
Rina Foygel Barber, Mathias Drton, Nils Sturma, and Luca Weihs (2022). Half-Trek Criterion for Identifiability of Latent Variable Models. Annals of Statistics 50(6):3174-3196.
Leopold Mareis, Nils Sturma, and Mathias Drton (2026). Semiparametric Inference for Half-Trek Estimators in Linear Structural Equation Models. arXiv:2606.26931.

tldr: The note's main surviving contribution is a conditional transfer template rather than a full constructive theorem on the TDE frontier. For a TDE-certified edge u -> v, a graph-uniform cumulant-admissible family C is a witness-specific augmentation that already makes each cumulant row factor through the relevant entry of B_Lambda; once such a family is supplied, the parent-effect row at v solves an observable linear system K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C), lambda_uv is the associated determinant ratio, and pointwise plug-in k-statistic inference follows at denominator-nonzero laws. The note then calibrates the limits of that template: finite-order admissible families need not exist for an arbitrary witness, and the witness-specific minimal transfer order m_star is not an information-theoretic lower identification frontier. The strongest unconditional positive theorem is narrower and covariance-layer: on the source-isolated subclass, the empty order-2 family is admissible, so the whole parent-effect vector is identified by a half-trek-style covariance determinant ratio that nests the classical IV formula when q=1.

project_justification.gap: The literature has the generic graphical certificate and hidden witness row system for direct effects under arbitrary bidirected confounding, plus covariance determinant formulas and special cumulant ratios on narrower subclasses, but it still lacks a clean account of which witness-specific observable lifts exist, how to package them once they do, and where such transfer constructions provably fail.
project_justification.niche: The niche here is not a new graphical criterion and not a blanket constructive theorem on the entire TDE frontier. It is a transfer analysis around that frontier: a conditional graph-to-cumulant lift for an arbitrary fixed witness, a calibration showing that finite-order admissible families need not exist, and a graph-legible covariance-order subclass where the lift becomes unconditional and lands in the closest determinant-ratio comparator class.
project_justification.fill: The proposal fills that niche by defining graph-uniform cumulant-admissible families as conditional transfer designs, proving the observable row system and determinant ratio once such a design is supplied, proving that witness-specific finite-order existence can fail, separating m_star from any global lower identification frontier, and establishing the source-isolated covariance theorem as the main unconditional positive subclass result.

related_work: Tramontano, Drton, and Etesami (2024) is the anchor because Theorem 3.8 gives the complete generic direct-effect criterion under general confounding and Eq. (3.9) exposes the hidden B_Lambda row system, but the paper estimates through residual-independence optimization rather than an observable formula. Kivva, Salehkaleybar, and Kiyavash (2023) provide the prototype single-proxy cross-moment ratio and the corresponding Gaussian impossibility result, so they are the cleanest one-row IV-style analogue of a cumulant ratio. Tramontano, Kivva, Salehkaleybar, Drton, and Kiyavash (2025) show in Sections 3.1 and 3.2 that higher-order cumulant equations can identify effects in single-proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM families, but those formulas are tailored to those subclasses rather than arbitrary ADMG witnesses with general bidirected confounding. Foygel, Draisma, and Drton (2012) and Barber, Drton, Sturma, and Weihs (2022) are the closest covariance-layer comparators: they already supply determinant-ratio and row-solving templates for half-trek-style systems, so the positive field-facing question here is not whether determinant algebra exists at covariance order, but which TDE witness subclasses can be shown to land in that comparator class by an explicit admissibility proof. Schkoda, Robeva, and Drton (2024) reinforce that graph-dependent cumulant matrices are the right observable objects for latent-confounded LiNGAM, yet their aim is discovery and finite compatible effect sets rather than a closed identifying functional for a TDE-certified edge. Mareis, Sturma, and Drton (2026) shows how semiparametric inference can be layered onto a rational SEM formula once the constructive estimand exists, which is the inferential template adapted here after the cumulant-transfer step.
honest_scope: The settled claims split cleanly into a conditional kernel, a narrower unconditional subclass theorem, and two calibration negatives. Conditionally, once an admissible cumulant family is supplied, the observable row system, determinant ratio, witness invariance, and pointwise delta-method inference follow. Unconditionally, the main positive theorem is the source-isolated covariance subclass, where the empty order-2 family is admissible and the observable lift collapses to a covariance determinant ratio for the whole parent-effect vector. Negatively, oeq:finite-order-transfer shows that the note's witness-specific search handle need not terminate, and oeq:minimal-order-sharpness shows that the witness-specific quantity m_star is not a global lower identification frontier. Those negative nodes calibrate the conditional kernel; they do not by themselves replace the need for the positive subclass result. Weak-denominator uniform coverage remains outside prop:plugin-delta-method.
